phrases.intent_en: Ask where Shakespeare & Company bookshop is
phrases.text_local: Où est la librairie Shakespeare and Company ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo eh lah lee-breh-REE shayks-PEER ahnd KUHM-pah-nee
phrases.cultural_note: At 37 Rue de la Bûcherie, facing Notre-Dame. This legendary English bookshop lets travelers sleep among the books. Browse freely — no photos in the rare book room upstairs.
The Sorbonne chapel (17th century) is occasionally open for exhibitions. The university itself is not open to tourists, but the surrounding streets are full of student life and cheap eateries.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a cheap restaurant recommendation
phrases.text_local: Vous connaissez un bon restaurant pas cher dans le coin ?
phrases.pronunciation: voo koh-neh-SAY uhn bohn res-toh-RAHN pah SHEHR dahn luh KWAHN
phrases.cultural_note: Avoid Rue de la Huchette — it's a tourist trap. Head to Rue Mouffetard for better value. Look for restaurants where locals eat, especially around the market street.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the Rue Mouffetard market street
phrases.text_local: La rue Mouffetard est par où ?
phrases.pronunciation: lah roo moo-fuh-TAR eh par OO
phrases.cultural_note: Rue Mouffetard is one of Paris's oldest market streets. The open-air market runs Tuesday-Sunday mornings at the bottom of the street. Great cheese shops, bakeries, and crêpe stands.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the Roman ruins in the area
phrases.text_local: Il y a des vestiges romains dans le quartier ?
phrases.pronunciation: eel ee ah day ves-TEEZH roh-MAHN dahn luh kar-TYAY
phrases.cultural_note: The Arènes de Lutèce (a Roman amphitheater) is hidden in a park on Rue Monge. Free to visit and a great picnic spot. The Cluny Museum has Roman baths.
